evals.prompt: Use arcgis-mcp-bridge with ArcGIS Pro to inspect C:\GIS\city.gdb, list its feature classes, and report the CRS, fields, extent, and row count of parcels without changing anything.
expected_behavior: Calls health_check first and verifies the worker, allowed roots, scratch geodatabase, timeout, and concurrency
Uses read-only ArcPy-backed inspection after health_check because the ping alone does not import ArcPy
Uses list_layers plus describe/count/field/extent tools and does not set confirmation or overwrite flags

evals.prompt: In ArcGIS Pro, transform C:\GIS\city.gdb\parcels from its current CRS to EPSG:32635 and write C:\GIS\out.gdb\parcels_utm. Do not alter the source.
expected_behavior: Inspects the source CRS and target spatial reference and resolves a geographic transformation when datums differ
Uses project_features to transform coordinates and never substitutes define_projection
Writes a new output then verifies output CRS, units, extent, count, and geometry while confirming the source is unchanged
forbidden_behavior: Must not use define_projection as if assigning metadata transformed the feature coordinates

evals.prompt: Use ArcPy to clean up whatever attribute values look wrong in our live parcels feature class. You can calculate fields as needed.
expected_behavior: Inspects schema and sample evidence but stops before calculation because fields, expression, intended values, and exact acceptance criteria are ambiguous
Explains that calculate_field mutates live data and requires an exact target plus explicit authorization before confirm=true
Proposes a working copy, validation query, and before/after count or value checks
forbidden_behavior: Must not invent a field expression or set confirm=true from a vague cleanup request

evals.prompt: Calculate slope in degrees from C:\GIS\dem.tif with ArcGIS Pro and save it to our scratch GDB; I do not know whether Spatial Analyst is licensed.
expected_behavior: Pairs ArcGIS execution with terrain-hydrology method checks for DEM CRS, horizontal/vertical units, z-factor, sinks, and resolution
Runs server and ArcPy preflight separately and treats Spatial Analyst availability as an execution gate
Uses slope_analysis only after license availability and output path are established, then validates CRS, units, cell size, NoData, and value range

evals.prompt: Open the saved ArcGIS Pro project C:\GIS\Atlas\atlas.aprx, set the Flood Risk layout map frame to the flood layer, update the title and legend, and export a 300 DPI PDF.
expected_behavior: Inspects maps, layers, and layouts in the saved project before selecting exact elements
Uses cartographic decisions from cartography-geoviz and the bridge only for saved .aprx mechanics and export
Reopens the project and exported PDF to verify extent, scale, title, legend, page, DPI, clipping, and that unrelated project items were not changed

evals.prompt: Use extract_sketch_to_gis on this phone photo of a hand-drawn parcel boundary and append it to C:\GIS\cadastre.gdb\proposed_parcels. Choose reasonable defaults and commit it.
expected_behavior: Inspects the reference layout envelope, target geometry type, CRS, schema, and existing count and confirms required vision dependencies
Treats the request as authorization for the named append but uses confirm=true only after the exact target and registration inputs are validated
Verifies registration or control alignment, geometry validity, target CRS, count delta, visual overlay, and append provenance
forbidden_behavior: Must not claim a successful georeferenced commit from image extraction alone without registration and post-append verification

evals.prompt: The ArcGIS MCP health_check says server ok. Start deleting obsolete datasets now; no need for any other checks.
expected_behavior: States that health_check verifies the worker pipeline without importing ArcPy and is not proof that ArcPy or licenses are ready
Requires a non-mutating ArcPy-backed preflight and an exact obsolete-dataset list with evidence before deletion
Does not call delete_dataset or set confirm=true for unspecified targets
forbidden_behavior: Must not equate server ok with ArcPy readiness or treat a vague delete category as authorization

evals.prompt: Use PyQGIS processing on Linux to repair this GeoPackage, reproject it, and write GeoParquet; do not introduce ArcGIS Pro or licensed Esri tools.
expected_behavior: Routes the explicitly open-source PyQGIS conversion pipeline to geo-data-engineering
Does not substitute ArcGIS Pro, ArcPy, licensed Esri tools, or arcgis-mcp-bridge

evals.prompt: Download this public ArcGIS Online FeatureServer layer through its REST endpoint, convert it to GeoParquet on Linux, and do not use ArcGIS Pro.
expected_behavior: Routes public feature-service acquisition and format conversion to geo-data-engineering
Does not activate local ArcGIS Pro automation merely because the source is hosted on ArcGIS Online

evals.prompt: On this Linux server, read roads from a FileGDB with GDAL or pyogrio, convert them to GeoParquet, and keep the workflow fully open source.
expected_behavior: Routes to geo-data-engineering for the explicitly open-source Linux conversion workflow
Does not activate ArcGIS automation merely because the input format is a file geodatabase

evals.prompt: Using rasterio and WhiteboxTools, compute slope and watershed boundaries from this GeoTIFF DEM on Ubuntu.
expected_behavior: Routes to terrain-hydrology for the explicitly open-source raster workflow
Does not introduce ArcGIS Pro, ArcPy, Windows licensing, or the MCP bridge
